etymology.description: 止(止) (zhǐ, 'foot') + 舟(舟) (zhōu, 'boat') = foot on a boat -> 前 (qián, 'forward') with 刀 (dāo, 'knife') added to form 剪 (jiǎn, 'cut')
etymology.interpretation: 歬(qián) was originally an ideogrammic compound representing the concept of a foot on a moving boat, indicating forward movement. It later evolved into the character 前(qián, 'forward'), which further incorporated 刀(dāo, 'knife') to form 剪(jiǎn, 'cut').
